document.title: Software Development Plan for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software development plan for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector software, document A-TCF-10-143 Rev.1 dated 2026-04-14.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by defining software development planning, classification, requirements, risk management, verification and validation, release, configuration management, and problem settlement activities.
keyPoints: The software development process follows Classic waterfall methodology.
Software is classified by FDA Level of Concern and by EN 62304 software safety classes.
Software requirements cover common functions, image acquisition, calibration, firmware update, iMX8, FPGA App, cyber security, and error control.
Software verification and validation includes unit test, integration and system test.
Software release requires completed verification, residual abnormality documentation and assessment, release version documentation, and controls for deployed software delivery.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This document is strictly private, confidential and intended solely for the use of H&abyz Co., Ltd.

entities.evidence: To use the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector developed, produced and sold by this company according to intention, it aims at developing software that can manipulate and utilize the product function.

concepts.name: Software Development Plan
concepts.evidence: This activity constructs the software development plan. This plan outlines the tasks, responsibilities, resource, and other items pertinent to the specific development project.

concepts.name: FDA Level of Concern
concepts.evidence: S/W is classified into Major, Moderate and Minor by its level of concern

concepts.name: EN 62304 software safety class
concepts.evidence: This company shall assign software safety class to each software system originated by our software system and based on the impact of the harm on the patient, the worker, or others.

concepts.name: Classic waterfall methodology
concepts.evidence: Software development process of H&abyz Co., Ltd. follows “Classic waterfall methodology"

concepts.name: Software Requirements Specification
concepts.evidence: Function by each module and essential factor that each function must equip in software are as below

concepts.name: Software Architectural Design
concepts.evidence: The software architectural design is a structure that explains the software requirements by the software structure and identifies the software item.

concepts.name: Software Design Specification
concepts.evidence: The software design specification explains materialization of the requirements on the software device.

concepts.name: Software Risk Management
concepts.evidence: As for the software risk management, personnel who participated in the program development process shall decide, analyze potential risk, reason, probability and severity of the software.

concepts.name: Software Verification and Validation
concepts.evidence: Software verification and validation shall be confirmed through the software test. The software test shall conduct the unit test, integration and system test.

concepts.name: Software Configuration Management
concepts.evidence: In case the software configuration is changed, it should be validated before it is applied to the software.

concepts.name: Cyber Security
concepts.evidence: Transmission data should be protected by applying encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, WPAPSK+TKIP, etc.

evidence.claim: The document is document number A-TCF-10-143, Rev.1.

evidence.claim: The document title is Software Development Plan for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.

evidence.claim: The report number is A-TCF-10-143 (Rev.1).
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-10-143 (Rev.1)

evidence.claim: The revision date is 2026-04-14.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Development Plan Rev. Date 2026-04-14

evidence.claim: Revision 0 was initially released on 2024.07.01 and revision 1 was a revision on 2026.04.14.
evidence.evidenceText: 0 2024.07.01 - Initial Release 1 2026.04.14 - Revision

evidence.claim: The document purpose is to develop software that can manipulate and utilize Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector product functions.
evidence.evidenceText: it aims at developing software that can manipulate and utilize the product function.

evidence.claim: Software is classified under FDA guidance into Major, Moderate, and Minor levels of concern.
evidence.evidenceText: S/W is classified into Major, Moderate and Minor by its level of concern

evidence.claim: EN 62304 software safety classes are Class A, Class B, and Class C.
evidence.evidenceText: Class A: There is no injury nor health damage. -. Class B: The possible harm is non-serious injury. -. Class C: The possible harm is death or serious injury.

evidence.claim: Until a software safety class is assigned, Class C requirements apply.
evidence.evidenceText: For each software system, until a software safety class is assigned, Class C requirements applied.

evidence.claim: The software development process follows Classic waterfall methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process of H&abyz Co., Ltd. follows “Classic waterfall methodology"

evidence.claim: The planned development stages include analysis, planning, design, implementation, qualification, maintenance, software release, and development plan update.
evidence.evidenceText: Step Main Contents Development Period Analysis Planning Design Implementation Qualification Maintenance Software release Development plan updated

evidence.claim: Hardware resources include a 32Bit Microprocessor and Intel Cyclone V MCU model.
evidence.evidenceText: MCU Function 32Bit Microprocessor MCU Model Intel Cyclone V

evidence.claim: Software resources include Windows 11 64bit and Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 Professional.
evidence.evidenceText: Operation system Windows 11 64bit Compiler Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 Professional / aarch64-poky-linux-gcc / C#

evidence.claim: Functional software requirements include common functions, image acquisition, calibration, and firmware update modules.
evidence.evidenceText: Common functions Module ... Image acquisition Module ... Calibration Module ... Firmware Update Module

evidence.claim: Firmware requirements include iMX8 and FPGA App modules.
evidence.evidenceText: Firmware function ① iMX8 Module ... ② FPGA App Module

evidence.claim: Cyber security requirements include wireless network ID and password controls.
evidence.evidenceText: Random access should be limited by setting ID and password when accessing the wireless network.

evidence.claim: Cyber security requirements include wireless transmission encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, and WPAPSK+TKIP.
evidence.evidenceText: Transmission data should be protected by applying encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, WPAPSK+TKIP, etc.

evidence.claim: Software risk analysis references EN ISO14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Software risk analysis Identification of the characteristics and hazards associated with the intended use and the safety of the device, risk assessment EN ISO14971:2019

evidence.claim: Software development planning references EN ISO13485:2016.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development Devise a plan for the software development and put it in writing EN ISO13485:2016

evidence.claim: Software risk management requires analyzing potential risk, reason, probability, and severity.
evidence.evidenceText: personnel who participated in the program development process shall decide, analyze potential risk, reason, probability and severity of the software.

evidence.claim: If risk remains in the non-acceptable zone after risk reduction or removal measures, design work is stopped.
evidence.evidenceText: If the risk still remains on the non-acceptable zone even after taking measures to reduce or remove the risk, stop the design work.

evidence.claim: For software hazards arising from failure to behave as specified, the probability of failure is assumed to be 100 percent under EN 62304:2006/AC:2008, Clause 4.3 a).
evidence.evidenceText: If the HAZARD could arise from a failure of the SOFTWARE SYSTEM to behave as specified, the probability of such failure shall be assumed to be 100 percent [∵EN 62304:2006/AC:2008, Cl. 4.3 a)].

evidence.claim: Software verification and validation is confirmed through software test, including unit test, integration, and system test.
evidence.evidenceText: Software verification and validation shall be confirmed through the software test. The software test shall conduct the unit test, integration and system test.

evidence.claim: Software release requires ensuring verification is complete and results are evaluated before release.
evidence.evidenceText: It should be ensured that the software verification is completed and that the results are evaluated before releasing the software.

evidence.claim: Identified residual abnormalities should be documented and evaluated to ensure they do not cause unacceptable risks.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual abnormalities should be documented. In order to ensure that all identified residual abnormalities are not causing unacceptable risks, It should be ensured that such abnormal conditions have been evaluated.

evidence.claim: The deployed software product version should be documented.
evidence.evidenceText: The version of the software product being deployed should be documented.

evidence.claim: Software configuration changes should be validated before application to the software.
evidence.evidenceText: In case the software configuration is changed, it should be validated before it is applied to the software.

evidence.claim: Software problems requiring change should be notified quickly to the R&D team.
evidence.evidenceText: if a software change is needed, it should quickly be notified to R&D team.

evidence.claim: Software shall be used only after required validation has completed when correcting software issues.
evidence.evidenceText: The software shall be used only after the required validation has completed.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal in metadata indicates MFDS/domestic, but the source text itself does not name MFDS; the document text references FDA guidance and EN/ISO standards.
Product signal CYAN appears in metadata and source path, while the document title identifies the product as G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
The source contains typographical and OCR-like issues, including misspellings such as detecotr and inconsistent spacing, but core metadata and technical content are legible.
